content.text:
The analysis is confirmed. This is a **fundamental architectural problem** — here's what's happening every time you add a wall:

---

## Root Cause: Full Scene Nuke-and-Rebuild on Every State Change

**`Viewport.tsx:1806–2062`** — there's a single `useEffect` that fires whenever `elementsById` changes (which happens on every edit). The very first thing it does:

```ts
// line 1811
while (root.children.length) root.remove(root.children[0]!);
```

It **destroys the entire 3D scene**, then rebuilds every single element from scratch — floors, walls, doors, windows, stairs, rooms, roofs, railings, sites — with **8 separate passes** over `Object.values(elementsById)`. This is O(N) work per every edit.

---

## The dependency array (lines 2054–2062) makes it worse

```ts
}, [
  elementsById,    // ← fires on every wall add
  selectedId,      // ← fires on every click/selection
  viewerCategoryHidden,
  viewerClipElevMm,
  viewerClipFloorElevMm,
  sectionBoxActive,
  theme,
]);
```

Even **selecting an element** triggers a full scene rebuild because `selectedId` is in the deps. With 50 elements that's manageable; with 200+ it becomes visibly sluggish.

---

## Secondary issue: Synchronous CSG operations

`makeWallWithOpenings` (line 778) runs synchronous boolean subtractions via `three-bvh-csg` for every wall that has hosted doors/windows. Each `evaluator.evaluate(wallBrush, cutter, SUBTRACTION)` blocks the main thread. As you accumulate more walls-with-openings, this cost grows linearly.

---

## Severity breakdown

| Issue | Impact |
|---|---|
| Nuke + rebuild full scene on `elementsById` change | **Critical** — O(N) per any edit |
| Nuke + rebuild on `selectedId` change | **High** — every click re-renders everything |
| Synchronous CSG per hosted opening | **High** — grows with wall count |
| 8 separate `Object.values()` passes | **Medium** — easy to consolidate |
| `buildSkyEnvMap` called every rebuild (line 2029) | **Medium** — expensive env map re-bake |

---

## The fix strategy

The right approach is **incremental scene management**: instead of rebuilding everything, maintain a `Map<elementId, THREE.Object3D>` and only add/update/remove the meshes that actually changed between `elementsById` snapshots. This turns O(N) rebuild into O(delta) per edit.

For `selectedId` specifically: no geometry rebuild is needed at all — just swap the material color on the affected mesh(es) in-place, which is a single material update.

Want me to implement the incremental scene manager? It's the highest-leverage change by far.

aiTitle: Analyze slow editing and wall addition performance

content.text:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

## What was implemented

**Root cause confirmed:** `Viewport.tsx` had a single `useEffect` that nuked the entire Three.js scene and rebuilt every mesh from scratch on every single state change — including every wall add, every click, every selection. O(N) synchronous work per edit.

### Tasks completed

**#1 Incremental scene manager** — The monolithic ~220-line rebuild effect is replaced with a diff-based manager using `bimPickMapRef` as a persistent mesh cache (`Map<elementId, THREE.Object3D>`). Only meshes that actually changed are torn down and rebuilt. Adding one wall → builds exactly 1 mesh instead of rebuilding everything.

**#2 Decoupled selectedId** — The `selectedId` was in the rebuild effect deps, meaning every click triggered a full scene rebuild. Now selection changes only update the outline-pass highlight (cheap property swap), not geometry.

**#3 Single-pass element iteration** — The 8 separate `Object.values(elementsById)` loops are replaced with one pass that buckets elements by kind and builds reverse dependency maps (`doorsByWall`, `winsByWall`, `railingsByStair`, `elemsByLevel`).

**#5 Env map rebuild** — Confirmed not present in the current codebase. The old analysis was looking at a different branch.

### Dependency propagation

The incremental manager correctly handles cascading invalidation:
- Door/window added → also marks host wall dirty (CSG opening must be cut)
- Door/window removed → same  
- Wall changes → marks its hosted doors/windows dirty
- Level elevation changes → marks all elements at that level dirty
- Stair changes → marks hosted railings dirty

**#4 (CSG Web Worker)** remains open — that's the next high-impact item once you want to pursue async CSG for walls with many openings.

content.text:
**#4 — Offload CSG to a Web Worker** is the remaining one. `makeWallWithOpenings` runs synchronous `three-bvh-csg` boolean subtractions on the main thread for every wall that has hosted doors or windows. As you accumulate those, each edit blocks the UI until all the cuts are done.

This one is significantly more complex to implement than the others — it requires:
1. A Worker that imports `three` + `three-bvh-csg` and computes the geometry
2. Transferring geometry buffers back via `postMessage` with transferable `ArrayBuffer`s
3. The main thread reassembling the `BufferGeometry` from those buffers and adding the mesh
4. A loading/placeholder mesh while the Worker is running

Want me to implement it now?

content.text:
All tasks done. Here's the full picture of what was implemented for task #4:

---

**New file: `src/viewport/csgWorker.ts`** — A Vite module worker that:
- Receives a `CsgRequest` message (plain number params: wall dims, door/window cut params, world pose)
- Runs the `three-bvh-csg` SUBTRACTION operations on its own thread
- Slices the resulting `BufferGeometry` attribute arrays into fresh `ArrayBuffer`s
- Transfers them back to the main thread via `postMessage` with the `transfer` option (zero-copy, no serialisation overhead)

**Viewport.tsx changes:**
- `makeWallWithOpenings` removed — CSG no longer runs synchronously on the main thread
- `Brush`, `Evaluator`, `SUBTRACTION` imports removed from Viewport
- Worker lifecycle: created in the mount effect, terminated in cleanup; reset on theme change
- **Nonce-based stale result discarding**: if a wall is edited twice quickly, the first job's result is silently dropped when the second arrives
- **Immediate placeholder**: when a wall has openings, a solid `makeWallMesh` is inserted instantly so the scene never shows a gap; the worker response swaps it for the CSG result when ready
- The worker response handler disposes the placeholder's GPU resources and inserts the reconstructed mesh into both the scene and the `bimPickMapRef` cache, then re-syncs the outline pass

content.content:
310	position and approximately overlap the wall volume. There is no actual
311	geometric opening. In the reference image, wall reveals around windows and
312	doors are visible as distinct surfaces.
313	
314	**What to build:**
315	
316	Use `THREE.CSG` (constructive solid geometry) via the `three-bvh-csg` package
317	(add to devDeps: `pnpm add three-bvh-csg`) to subtract door and window
318	volumes from the wall.
319	
320	```ts
321	import { Brush, Evaluator, SUBTRACTION } from 'three-bvh-csg';
322	
323	function makeWallWithOpenings(
324	  wall: WallElem,
325	  hostedElements: (DoorElem | WindowElem)[],
326	  elevM: number,
327	  paint: ViewportPaintBundle | null,
328	): THREE.Mesh {
329	  // 1. Build the solid wall brush (same geometry as makeWallMesh today)
330	  const wallBrush = new Brush(wallGeometry, wallMaterial);
331	  wallBrush.updateMatrixWorld();
332	
333	  // 2. For each hosted door/window, build a cutter brush
334	  for (const hosted of hostedElements) {
335	    const cutterGeom  = hosted.kind === 'door'
336	      ? doorCutterGeometry(hosted, wall)
337	      : windowCutterGeometry(hosted, wall);
338	    const cutterBrush = new Brush(cutterGeom, wallMaterial);
339	    cutterBrush.updateMatrixWorld();
340	    const evaluator = new Evaluator();
341	    wallBrush.geometry = evaluator.evaluate(
342	      wallBrush, cutterBrush, SUBTRACTION
343	    ).geometry;
344	  }
345	  const result = new THREE.Mesh(wallBrush.geometry, wallMaterial);
346	  result.userData.bimPickId = wall.id;
347	  return result;
348	}
349	```
350	
351	**Cutter volumes:**
352	- Door cutter: same `(width, height, thickness + 0.1)` box as today's door
353	  proxy, placed at `hostedXZ(door, wall)` at `elevM` (floor level) so the
354	  cut goes to the sill.
355	- Window cutter: `(width, height, thickness + 0.1)` box placed at
356	  `elevM + sillHeight`.
357	
358	**Performance:** CSG is O(n·m) in polygon count. Gate it behind a
359	`ENABLE_CSG` flag (`import.meta.env.VITE_ENABLE_CSG === 'true'`); default
360	off until R2-02 and R2-03 deliver the frame/reveal geometry that makes the
361	cuts visible and worth the cost.
362	
363	**Acceptance:** Looking at a wall face-on, the door and window openings are
364	visible as holes through the wall. The wall edges around each opening are
365	visible as separate geometry lines from `EdgesGeometry`. Interior wall
366	reveal surfaces are a separate face receiving AO.
367

# Rendering V2 — Architectural Visualization Spec

**Goal:** Match the visual quality of a Revit hidden-line + shaded perspective:
directional shadow, ambient occlusion in concave joins, crisp edge lines on
every element, correct PBR materials (glazing, metal, concrete, timber), and
real architectural geometry (door frames, window reveals, railing balusters,
stair treads, correct roof mesh from footprint polygon).

**Status baseline (2026-05-06):** All geometry is Three.js `BoxGeometry` or
hand-coded flat `BufferGeometry` proxies. Shadows, AO, and edge lines are
fully absent. Directional light is at a hardcoded world position. The
`EffectComposer` and post-processing chain do not exist.

**Primary file:** `packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`  
**Rendering support:** `packages/web/src/viewport/materials.ts`  
**Camera:** `packages/web/src/viewport/cameraRig.ts`

---

## Phase R1 — Rendering Pipeline Foundation

These four work packages wire the post-processing chain and enable shadow
maps. They are independent of geometry quality and deliver immediate visual
improvement on the existing box-proxy meshes.

---

### R1-01 · Shadow map enable

**What the image shows:** Hard directional shadows cast by the building volume
onto the site slab; each element casts and receives.

**What exists today:**  
`resolveLighting()` in `materials.ts` defines `shadowMapSize: 2048` but the
value is never read. The `DirectionalLight` is created without `castShadow`.
No mesh sets `castShadow` or `receiveShadow`. `renderer.shadowMap.enabled`
is never called.

**What to build:**

1. **Enable the shadow map on the renderer** immediately after construction
   (Viewport.tsx, near `renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer(...)`):
   ```ts
   renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true;
   renderer.shadowMap.type = THREE.PCFSoftShadowMap;
   ```
   `PCFSoftShadowMap` gives soft penumbra edges that match the reference
   image without the harsh aliasing of `BasicShadowMap`.

2. **Configure the DirectionalLight shadow camera** when wiring the paint

## Performance architecture (implemented 2026-05-06)

### Problem

The original Viewport had a single monolithic `useEffect` that **nuked the
entire Three.js scene and rebuilt every mesh synchronously on every state
change** — including every element add, every selection click, and every
category toggle. This was O(N) geometry work per edit, blocking the main
thread until complete.

Additionally, `makeWallWithOpenings` (CSG boolean subtraction) ran synchronously
on the main thread for every wall that hosted doors or windows, blocking the UI
for hundreds of milliseconds on complex models.

### Solution

**Incremental scene manager** (`Viewport.tsx` — geometry `useEffect`):

- `bimPickMapRef` (`Map<elementId, THREE.Object3D>`) serves as the persistent
  mesh cache. Meshes survive across renders; only changed elements are touched.
- On each `elementsById` change the effect **diffs** against `prevElementsByIdRef`
  to compute `addedIds`, `removedIds`, and `changedIds`.
- **Dependency propagation** ensures cascading invalidation:
  - Wall changed → hosted doors + windows also dirty (positions depend on wall)
  - Door/window added/changed/removed → host wall dirty (CSG opening changes)
  - Level elevation changed → all elements at that level dirty
  - Stair changed → hosted railings dirty
- Old meshes in `toRemove` are disposed (geometry + materials) to avoid GPU
  leaks. New meshes for `toRebuild` are built and inserted.
- Result: **O(delta) per edit** instead of O(N).

**Selection decoupled from geometry** (`[selectedId]` effect):

- The `selectedId` is no longer in the geometry effect's dependency array.
- Selection changes only update the OutlinePass object list — no mesh is
  rebuilt. This makes click-to-select free from a geometry standpoint.

**Category visibility** (`viewerCategoryHidden` in geometry effect):

- `mesh.visible` is toggled via a reference-equality check against the previous
  `catHidden` object. Only a sweep over cached meshes occurs — no geometry
  rebuild.

**Clipping planes** (separate `[viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm,
sectionBoxActive]` effect):

- Clipping plane changes no longer trigger geometry reconstruction. The
  dedicated effect applies `THREE.Plane` clipping to materials on all existing
  meshes, and manages the section-box wireframe cage independently.

**CSG Web Worker** (`src/viewport/csgWorker.ts`):

- `three-bvh-csg` SUBTRACTION operations run in a dedicated `Worker` instead of
  on the main thread.
- The main thread sends a plain-number `CsgRequest` message (wall dims, cutter
  params, world pose) and immediately inserts a solid `makeWallMesh` placeholder.
- The worker transfers `Float32Array` / `Uint32Array` geometry buffers back via
  `postMessage` with the `transfer` option (zero-copy). The main thread
  reassembles the `BufferGeometry`, disposes the placeholder, and inserts the
  CSG mesh.
- **Nonce-based stale result discarding**: each wall tracks an active job nonce
  in `pendingCsgRef`. If a wall is edited again before its CSG job returns, the
  response is silently dropped.
- Worker lifecycle is tied to the mount effect: created on mount, terminated
  (and cache cleared) on unmount or theme change.

### Effect dependency summary after refactor

| Effect | Dependencies | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| Mount (renderer + scene) | `[theme]` | Initial mount; theme switch |
| Incremental geometry | `[elementsById, viewerCategoryHidden, theme]` | Element add/change/remove; category toggle |
| Clipping + section box | `[viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm, sectionBoxActive]` | Clip plane change; section box toggle |
| Selection outline | `[selectedId]` | Click to select/deselect |
| Orbit camera | `[orbitCameraNonce, orbitCameraPoseMm]` | Saved viewpoint applied |
| Walk mode | `[walkActive]` | Walk mode toggle |
| Section box sync | `[sectionBoxActive]` | Section box toggle |

  the OutlinePass, no mesh rebuild
- Category visibility handled via mesh.visible sweep (reference equality
  check), not geometry reconstruction
- Clipping planes + section-box cage extracted into their own effect
  [viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm, sectionBoxActive]

CSG offloaded to Web Worker (src/viewport/csgWorker.ts):
- three-bvh-csg SUBTRACTION runs in a dedicated Worker, never blocking
  the main thread
- Main thread shows a solid-wall placeholder immediately; worker
  transfers Float32Array/Uint32Array geometry buffers back zero-copy
- Nonce-based stale result discarding: if a wall is edited again before
  its CSG job returns the response is silently dropped
- Worker lifecycle tied to the mount effect; terminated on unmount/theme

spec/rendering-v2-spec.md: added performance architecture section
documenting the incremental design, effect dependency table, and
worker protocol.

1	# Rendering V2 — Architectural Visualization Spec
2	
3	**Goal:** Match the visual quality of a Revit hidden-line + shaded perspective:
4	directional shadow, ambient occlusion in concave joins, crisp edge lines on
5	every element, correct PBR materials (glazing, metal, concrete, timber), and
6	real architectural geometry (door frames, window reveals, railing balusters,
7	stair treads, correct roof mesh from footprint polygon).
8	
9	**Status baseline (2026-05-06):** All geometry is Three.js `BoxGeometry` or
10	hand-coded flat `BufferGeometry` proxies. Shadows, AO, and edge lines are
11	fully absent. Directional light is at a hardcoded world position. The
12	`EffectComposer` and post-processing chain do not exist.
13	
14	**Primary file:** `packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`  
15	**Rendering support:** `packages/web/src/viewport/materials.ts`  
16	**Camera:** `packages/web/src/viewport/cameraRig.ts`
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Phase R1 — Rendering Pipeline Foundation
21	
22	These four work packages wire the post-processing chain and enable shadow
23	maps. They are independent of geometry quality and deliver immediate visual
24	improvement on the existing box-proxy meshes.
25	
26	---
27	
28	### R1-01 · Shadow map enable
29	
30	**What the image shows:** Hard directional shadows cast by the building volume
31	onto the site slab; each element casts and receives.
32	
33	**What exists today:**  
34	`resolveLighting()` in `materials.ts` defines `shadowMapSize: 2048` but the
35	value is never read. The `DirectionalLight` is created without `castShadow`.
36	No mesh sets `castShadow` or `receiveShadow`. `renderer.shadowMap.enabled`
37	is never called.
38	
39	**What to build:**
40	
41	1. **Enable the shadow map on the renderer** immediately after construction
42	   (Viewport.tsx, near `renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer(...)`):
43	   ```ts
44	   renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true;
45	   renderer.shadowMap.type = THREE.PCFSoftShadowMap;
46	   ```
47	   `PCFSoftShadowMap` gives soft penumbra edges that match the reference
48	   image without the harsh aliasing of `BasicShadowMap`.
49	
50	2. **Configure the DirectionalLight shadow camera** when wiring the paint
51	   bundle (currently lines 689–694 of Viewport.tsx):
52	   ```ts
53	   sun.castShadow = true;
54	   sun.shadow.mapSize.set(
55	     paint.lighting.sun.shadowMapSize,   // 2048
56	     paint.lighting.sun.shadowMapSize,
57	   );
58	   // Orthographic frustum must enclose the whole building.
59	   // Use the model bounding box (recomputed on scene rebuild).
60	   const frustumHalf = Math.max(sceneRadiusM * 1.2, 20);
61	   sun.shadow.camera.left   = -frustumHalf;
62	   sun.shadow.camera.right  =  frustumHalf;
63	   sun.shadow.camera.top    =  frustumHalf;
64	   sun.shadow.camera.bottom = -frustumHalf;
65	   sun.shadow.camera.near   = 0.5;
66	   sun.shadow.camera.far    = sceneRadiusM * 4 + 50;
67	   sun.shadow.bias = -0.001;   // eliminates shadow acne on flat surfaces
68	   ```
69	   `sceneRadiusM` = half the diagonal of the scene AABB, computed after
70	   all meshes are added.
71	
72	3. **Tag every mesh** returned by the factory functions
73	   (`makeWallMesh`, `makeFloorSlabMesh`, `makeDoorMesh`, `makeWindowMesh`,
74	   `makeStairVolumeMesh`, `makeRailingMesh`, `makeRoofMassMesh`,
75	   `makeSiteMesh`):
76	   ```ts
77	   mesh.castShadow    = true;
78	   mesh.receiveShadow = true;
79	   ```
80	   Site slab should have `castShadow = false` (it sits on the ground and
81	   casts no visible shadow onto anything below it).
82	
83	4. **Recompute shadow camera frustum** on every scene rebuild (the scene is
84	   rebuilt when `elementsById` changes). After adding all meshes, compute the
85	   scene AABB and update `sun.shadow.camera.left/right/top/bottom/far`.
86	
87	**Acceptance:** The site slab receives a hard but soft-edged shadow from the
88	building mass. Walls cast shadows on each other and on the floor. The shadow
89	moves when the camera orbits (shadows are view-independent). No shadow acne
90	on flat horizontal surfaces.
91	
92	**Vitest:** Unit-test `resolveLighting()` to assert `shadowMapSize` is 2048
93	and is present (it already is). Integration coverage is via Playwright
94	screenshot diff — add a `shadow-enabled.png` baseline to
95	`e2e/ui-redesign-baselines.spec.ts`.
96	
97	---
98	
99	### R1-02 · Directional light from azimuth + elevation
100	
101	**What exists today:**  
102	`resolveLighting()` returns `{ azimuthDeg: 145, elevationDeg: 35 }` but the
103	light is placed at a hardcoded world position `(8, 12, 6)` (Viewport.tsx line
104	near `sun.position.set(8, 12, 6)`). The spec-defined azimuth and elevation
105	are never used.
106	
107	**What to build:**
108	
109	Replace the hardcoded `position.set` call with a spherical → Cartesian
110	conversion using the values from the paint bundle:
111	```ts
112	function sunPositionFromAzEl(
113	  azimuthDeg: number,
114	  elevationDeg: number,
115	  radiusM = 80,             // large enough to be "at infinity" for the scene
116	): THREE.Vector3 {
117	  const az  = THREE.MathUtils.degToRad(azimuthDeg);
118	  const el  = THREE.MathUtils.degToRad(elevationDeg);
119	  return new THREE.Vector3(
120	    radiusM * Math.cos(el) * Math.sin(az),
121	    radiusM * Math.sin(el),
122	    radiusM * Math.cos(el) * Math.cos(az),
123	  );
124	}
125	
126	sun.position.copy(sunPositionFromAzEl(
127	  paint.lighting.sun.azimuthDeg,
128	  paint.lighting.sun.elevationDeg,
129	));
130	sun.target.position.set(0, 0, 0);
131	```
132

134	`materials.ts` (they already exist in the return value of `resolveLighting()`

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user reported that editing operations (e.g., adding a wall) were noticeably slow and requested a deep analysis of the performance issue. Subsequent requests were: (a) implement fixes and track them via a task tracker, (b) implement the remaining task (CSG Web Worker), (c) confirm everything is implemented, committed/pushed, and documented. The session ended with a question about whether everything was pushed and tracked documentation-wise.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Three.js incremental scene management (mesh cache, diff-based updates)
   - React `useEffect` dependency splitting for O(delta) vs O(N) rendering
   - CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) via `three-bvh-csg` package
   - Web Workers with transferable `ArrayBuffer`s (zero-copy geometry transfer)
   - Nonce-based stale job discarding for async worker results
   - `bimPickMapRef` as a persistent `Map<elementId, THREE.Object3D>` mesh cache
   - Zustand state store and reference equality for diff detection
   - `OutlinePass` for selection highlighting (post-processing, not material swap)
   - Dependency propagation graph: wall↔doors/windows, level→all-at-level, stair→railings
   - GPU resource disposal (geometry + materials) on mesh removal
   - TypeScript Web Worker typing without the `WebWorker` lib (using `self as unknown as {...}`)
   - Vite module worker pattern: `new Worker(new URL(...), { type: 'module' })`

     - **New refs** added after `bimPickMapRef`:

       ```typescript
       const clippingPlanesRef = useRef<THREE.Plane[]>([]);
       const selectedIdRef = useRef<string | undefined>(undefined);
       const prevCatHiddenRef = useRef<Record<string, boolean>>({});
       const csgWorkerRef = useRef<Worker | null>(null);
       const pendingCsgRef = useRef<Map<string, number>>(new Map());
       const csgNonceRef = useRef(0);
       ```
     - **After selectedId selector**: `selectedIdRef.current = selectedId;`
     - **Mount effect cleanup** additions:
       ```typescript
       bimPickMapRef.current = new Map();
       prevElementsByIdRef.current = {};
       hasAutoFittedRef.current = false;
       csgWorkerRef.current?.terminate();
       csgWorkerRef.current = null;
       pendingCsgRef.current.clear();
       ```
     - **Worker initialization** in mount effect after `sectionBoxRef.current = sectionBox`:
       ```typescript
       const csgWorker = new Worker(new URL('./viewport/csgWorker.ts', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' });
       csgWorkerRef.current = csgWorker;
       csgWorker.onmessage = (evt: MessageEvent<CsgResponse>) => {
       ```

       2. Clipping + section-box effect `[viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm, sectionBoxActive]`
     - **Wall case in geometry effect** (async CSG dispatch):
       ```typescript
       case 'wall': {
         const elev = elevationMForLevel(e.levelId, curr);
         const doors = doorsByWall.get(id) ?? [];
         const wins = winsByWall.get(id) ?? [];
         if (CSG_ENABLED && (doors.length > 0 || wins.length > 0)) {
           const nonce = ++csgNonceRef.current;
           pendingCsgRef.current.set(id, nonce);
           csgWorkerRef.current?.postMessage(job);
         }
         obj = makeWallMesh(e, elev, paint); // placeholder
         break;
       }
       ```

     - Exports types: `DoorCutParams`, `WindowCutParams`, `CsgRequest`, `CsgResponse`
     - Uses `const ctx = self as unknown as { onmessage: ...; postMessage: ... }` to avoid needing WebWorker lib
     - Reuses a single `Evaluator` instance (module-level, not per-request)
     - Slices geometry arrays before transferring: `(geom.getAttribute('position').array as Float32Array).slice()`
     - Transfers via `ctx.postMessage(response, { transfer: transferables })`
     - Falls back to `{ ok: false }` response on error
     ```typescript

       onmessage: ((evt: MessageEvent<CsgRequest>) => void) | null;
       postMessage(message: CsgResponse, options?: { transfer?: Transferable[] }): void;
     };

     ```

     - Appended a "Performance architecture (implemented 2026-05-06)" section before the final `---`
     - Documents: problem statement (O(N) nuke-rebuild), incremental scene manager design, dependency propagation rules, CSG worker protocol, effect dependency table

4. Errors and Fixes:

   - **TypeScript error: `DedicatedWorkerGlobalScope` not in scope** — Initial attempt used `declare const self: DedicatedWorkerGlobalScope` but that type isn't available without WebWorker lib.
     - Fix: Used the `ctx` cast approach instead (no need for the type name).
   - **Working tree changes wiped by branch switch** — Background Entire agents merged branches `r3-01-pbr-tuning` and `r3-04-outline-selection` into `main`, which involved a `checkout: moving from r3-04-outline-selection to main` that discarded all unstaged working tree modifications. All Viewport.tsx, csgWorker.ts, and spec changes were lost.

`postMessage` with the `transfer` option (zero-copy). The main thread
  reassembles the `BufferGeometry`, disposes the placeholder, and inserts the
  CSG mesh.
- **Nonce-based stale result discarding**: each wall tracks an active job nonce
  in `pendingCsgRef`. If a wall is edited again before its CSG job returns, the
  response is silently dropped.
- Worker lifecycle is tied to the mount effect: created on mount, terminated
  (and cache cleared) on unmount or theme change.

### Effect dependency summary after refactor

| Effect | Dependencies | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| Mount (renderer + scene) | `[theme]` | Initial mount; theme switch |
| Incremental geometry | `[elementsById, viewerCategoryHidden, theme]` | Element add/change/remove; category toggle |
| Clipping + section box | `[viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm, sectionBoxActive]` | Clip plane change; section box toggle |
| Selection outline | `[selectedId]` | Click to select/deselect |
| Orbit camera | `[orbitCameraNonce, orbitCameraPoseMm]` | Saved viewpoint applied |
| Walk mode | `[walkActive]` | Walk mode toggle |
| Section box sync | `[sectionBoxActive]` | Section box toggle |

---

### FL — Implementation notes

**Where families live:**
- Built-in catalog: `packages/web/src/families/familyCatalog.ts` — static
  module, zero runtime cost, tree-shaken for families not used
- User-defined types: in `elementsById` (project model) as `family_type`
  elements with `isBuiltIn: false`
- Geometry functions: `packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/` — one file per
  discipline (`doorGeometry.ts`, `windowGeometry.ts`, etc.)

**Parameter type safety:**

Each `FamilyDefinition.params` array is the schema. A `validateTypeParams`
function checks all required keys are present and within bounds before saving.
The renderer's `resolveElementParams` never throws — it always has a fallback.

**Extensibility:**

The catalog is designed to be extended by:
1. Adding entries to `BUILT_IN_FAMILIES` in `familyCatalog.ts`
2. Adding a geometry function to `geometryFns/`
3. Wiring the family ID into the renderer dispatch table

A third-party library of families (e.g. a furniture catalog, structural
columns) can be added as a separate npm package that exports a
`FamilyDefinition[]` array — the browser merges it with `BUILT_IN_FAMILIES`
at startup. No server required.
